confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_7b649a68-e706-45d7-88de-f6b1b1c42d8a read back as received

payload.doctrinal_relevance: Narada continuity is not only textual or database state; inhabited work also has spatial and UI embodiment. Site embodiments and resume machinery should be able to name, launch, focus, and recover the operator surfaces where work is inhabited.

evidence.komorebi_effect: Komorebi can address the resulting window by stable title instead of unstable HWND or prompt-mutated shell title.
evidence.operator_need: Narada operator work involves recurring role windows such as narada-proper, narada-andrey, staccato-narada, thoughts-architect, and client-service Sites. These should be stable launchable surfaces rather than incidental terminal tabs.

proposal.definition: A durable, addressable interface through which a human operator or agent inhabits a Narada Site, role, workflow, or operational locus.

payload.proposed_direction: Document Operator Surface in Narada proper, relate it to Site embodiments/resume/console/operator ergonomics, and consider machinery that lets Sites declare operator surfaces with adapter-specific materializers for Windows Terminal, Komorebi, YASB, and future environments.
payload.summary: While stabilizing the Windows operator workflow, we discovered that Windows Terminal profiles with explicit tabTitle and suppressApplicationTitle provide durable, addressable windows that Komorebi/YASB/AHK can target. The underlying Narada concept is not a terminal profile, but an Operator Surface: a durable, addressable interface through which a human operator or agent inhabits a Site, role, or workflow.
payload.title: Introduce Operator Surface as a first-class Narada construct
